import json

# Load the file
file_path = locals()['var_function-call-16498150637540614880']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    articles = json.load(f)

# Define sports keywords
sports_keywords = [
    "olympic", "athens", "medal", "gold", "silver", "bronze", 
    "basketball", "nba", "baseball", "mlb", "football", "nfl", 
    "soccer", "premier league", "tennis", "wimbledon", "us open", 
    "golf", "pga", "hockey", "nhl", "cricket", "rugby", "f1", "nascar",
    "championship", "tournament", "world cup", "athlete", "stadium",
    "yankees", "red sox", "lakers", "spurs", "pistons", "armstrong", "phelps", "thorpe"
]

candidates = []

for art in articles:
    title = art.get('title', '')
    desc = art.get('description', '')
    text = (title + " " + desc).lower()
    
    # Check if any keyword is in text
    is_sport = False
    for kw in sports_keywords:
        # Simple containment check; for better accuracy, could use word boundaries
        if kw in text:
            # Filter out some false positives if necessary
            # e.g. "gold" might be in business (gold prices)
            if kw == "gold" or kw == "silver" or kw == "bronze":
                if "price" in text or "market" in text:
                    continue
            if kw == "medal" and "honor" in text: # Medal of honor?
                pass
                
            is_sport = True
            break
            
    if is_sport:
        candidates.append({
            "title": title,
            "desc_len": len(desc),
            "description": desc
        })

# Sort by description length descending
candidates.sort(key=lambda x: x['desc_len'], reverse=True)

# Print top 5 candidates to verify
print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(candidates[:5]))

import json
import re

file_path = locals()['var_function-call-16498150637540614880']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    articles = json.load(f)

sports_keywords = {
    "olympic", "olympics", "athens", "athlete", "athletics", "medal", "medals",
    "basketball", "nba", "knicks", "lakers", "celtics", "spurs", "pistons", "bulls", "heat",
    "baseball", "mlb", "yankees", "red sox", "mets", "dodgers", "cubs", "cardinals", "braves", "marlins", "giants",
    "football", "nfl", "quarterback", "touchdown", "super bowl", "patriots", "eagles", "colts", "steelers",
    "soccer", "fifa", "uefa", "premier league", "manchester", "arsenal", "liverpool", "real madrid", "barcelona", "chelsea",
    "tennis", "wimbledon", "us open", "grand slam", "agassi", "roddick", "federer", "serena", "venus", "sharapova",
    "golf", "pga", "tiger woods", "masters", "ryder cup",
    "hockey", "nhl", "stanley cup",
    "cycling", "tour de france", "lance armstrong",
    "swimming", "phelps", "thorpe",
    "gymnastics", "marathon", "sprint",
    "f1", "nascar", "racing", "driver", "grand prix",
    "boxing", "wrestling", "championship", "tournament", "world cup"
}

candidates = []

for art in articles:
    title = art.get('title', '')
    desc = art.get('description', '')
    text = (title + " " + desc).lower()
    
    # Tokenize simply
    tokens = set(re.findall(r'[a-z0-9]+', text))
    
    matched = tokens.intersection(sports_keywords)
    
    # Logic for "driver" and "racing" and "gold" etc
    # If only weak keywords match, check context
    # But I removed weak keywords from the set above (except "driver", "racing", "medal")
    
    if matched:
        # Check for false positives
        is_sport = True
        
        # If the only match is "driver", check for tech context
        if matched == {"driver"}:
            if any(w in tokens for w in ["software", "hardware", "computer", "printer", "device"]):
                is_sport = False
                
        # If the only match is "racing", check for "heart"
        if matched == {"racing"}:
            if "heart" in tokens:
                is_sport = False

        # If only match is "medal"/"medals", check for "honor"
        if matched.issubset({"medal", "medals"}):
            if "honor" in tokens:
                is_sport = False

        if is_sport:
            candidates.append({
                "title": title,
                "desc_len": len(desc),
                "description": desc,
                "matched": list(matched)
            })

# Sort
candidates.sort(key=lambda x: x['desc_len'], reverse=True)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(candidates[:10]))

import json
import re

file_path = locals()['var_function-call-2751355608514880236']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    articles = json.load(f)

positive_keywords = {
    "olympic", "olympics", "athens", "medal", "medals", "basketball", "nba", 
    "baseball", "mlb", "football", "nfl", "soccer", "tennis", "golf", "pga", 
    "hockey", "nhl", "f1", "nascar", "boxing", "wrestling", "championship", 
    "tournament", "world cup", "swimming", "gymnastics", "marathon", "sprint", 
    "relay", "freestyle", "touchdown", "quarterback", "goal", "puck", "court", 
    "stadium", "athlete", "team", "teams", "coach", "score", "scored", "win", 
    "won", "winning", "lost", "loss", "beat", "defeat", "victory", "match", 
    "game", "games", "play", "played", "player", "players", "sport", "sports",
    "yankees", "red sox", "mets", "lakers", "spurs", "pistons", "bulls",
    "phelps", "thorpe", "armstrong", "agassi", "roddick", "federer", "woods"
}

negative_keywords = {
    "technology", "tech", "security", "software", "hardware", "internet", "web", 
    "google", "microsoft", "intel", "amd", "chips", "satellite", "space", "nasa", 
    "orbit", "market", "stock", "stocks", "wall", "street", "dow", "nasdaq", 
    "investor", "investors", "profit", "earnings", "sales", "economy", "economic", 
    "oil", "price", "prices", "iraq", "war", "military", "soldier", "troops", 
    "president", "election", "kerry", "bush", "politics", "refugee", "un", "imf", 
    "bank", "dollar", "euro", "yen", "video", "console", "ps2", "xbox", "nintendo",
    "movie", "film", "actor", "drama", "show", "television", "tv", "entertainment"
}

candidates = []

for art in articles:
    title = art.get('title', '')
    desc = art.get('description', '')
    text = (title + " " + desc).lower()
    
    words = re.findall(r'\w+', text)
    
    score = 0
    for w in words:
        if w in positive_keywords:
            score += 1
        if w in negative_keywords:
            score -= 5 # Heavy penalty to filter out Business/Tech/World
            
    # Heuristic for "race"
    if "race" in words:
        # Check context
        if "election" in words or "president" in words:
            score -= 5
        elif "f1" in words or "nascar" in words or "swimming" in words or "relay" in words:
            score += 1
            
    if score >= 3: # Reduced threshold? Or keep strict.
        candidates.append({
            "title": title,
            "desc_len": len(desc),
            "description": desc,
            "score": score
        })

# Sort by length
candidates.sort(key=lambda x: x['desc_len'], reverse=True)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(candidates[:10]))

import json
import re

file_path = locals()['var_function-call-2751355608514880236']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    articles = json.load(f)

positive_keywords = {
    "olympic", "olympics", "athens", "medal", "medals", "basketball", "nba", 
    "baseball", "mlb", "football", "nfl", "soccer", "tennis", "golf", "pga", 
    "hockey", "nhl", "f1", "nascar", "boxing", "wrestling", "championship", 
    "tournament", "world cup", "swimming", "gymnastics", "marathon", "sprint", 
    "relay", "freestyle", "touchdown", "quarterback", "goal", "puck", "court", 
    "stadium", "athlete", "team", "teams", "coach", "score", "scored", "win", 
    "won", "winning", "lost", "loss", "beat", "defeat", "victory", "match", 
    "game", "games", "play", "played", "player", "players", "sport", "sports",
    "yankees", "red sox", "mets", "lakers", "spurs", "pistons", "bulls",
    "phelps", "thorpe", "armstrong", "agassi", "roddick", "federer", "woods"
}

negative_keywords = {
    "technology", "tech", "security", "software", "hardware", "internet", "web", 
    "google", "microsoft", "intel", "amd", "chips", "satellite", "space", "nasa", 
    "orbit", "market", "stock", "stocks", "wall", "street", "dow", "nasdaq", 
    "investor", "investors", "profit", "earnings", "sales", "economy", "economic", 
    "oil", "price", "prices", "iraq", "war", "military", "soldier", "troops", 
    "president", "election", "kerry", "bush", "politics", "refugee", "un", "imf", 
    "bank", "dollar", "euro", "yen", "video", "console", "ps2", "xbox", "nintendo",
    "movie", "film", "actor", "drama", "show", "television", "tv", "entertainment"
}

candidates = []

for art in articles:
    title = art.get('title', '')
    desc = art.get('description', '')
    text = (title + " " + desc).lower()
    
    words = re.findall(r'\w+', text)
    
    score = 0
    for w in words:
        if w in positive_keywords:
            score += 1
        if w in negative_keywords:
            score -= 5 # Heavy penalty to filter out Business/Tech/World
            
    # Heuristic for "race"
    if "race" in words:
        # Check context
        if "election" in words or "president" in words:
            score -= 5
        elif "f1" in words or "nascar" in words or "swimming" in words or "relay" in words:
            score += 1
            
    if score >= 3: # Reduced threshold? Or keep strict.
        candidates.append({
            "title": title,
            "desc_len": len(desc),
            "description": desc,
            "score": score
        })

# Sort by length
candidates.sort(key=lambda x: x['desc_len'], reverse=True)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(candidates[:10]))
